meta.setting: Klamath River gravel bar and forested canyon country
meta.rdr_clone_target: PS3-era RDR-clone western art, grounded in river work, stewardship, and rugged travel

research_grounding.title: Karuk Tribe
research_grounding.use: Klamath River homeland, salmon fishing, basketry, environmental stewardship, cultural burning, and self-governance.

research_grounding.title: Yurok Tribe
research_grounding.use: Land and water stewardship, self-governance, cultural preservation, reciprocity, and dignity.

image_prompt.subject: Karuk river families working near a Klamath River gravel bar with salmon gear, baskets, pack animals, and a traveler waiting respectfully at distance
image_prompt.scene: clear river light in a forested canyon; smoke from a careful burn far above the water, practical 1899 work clothing, pack loads set down, children and elders included naturally, no ceremonial staging
image_prompt.composition: square scene with the river curve leading through people, baskets, pack animals, and gear; the outsider is visibly a guest, not the center
image_prompt.camera: mid-distance bank view with respectful spacing, early-HD environmental storytelling, and no trophy framing
material_cues: river cobbles and clear water glare
basket texture and worn pack straps
forest smoke haze far up slope
image_prompt.hand_cut_note: The outsider should look like the guest; the river families should look like the people who know the map.

style_modifiers.medium: PS3-era open-world western game art, early-HD community/environment scene
render_traits: slightly simplified but respectful human and animal silhouettes
modest texture resolution on water, baskets, cloth, leather, and stone
hard ambient occlusion under packs, baskets, animals, and river stones
clear river daylight with forest-shadow contrast
believable 1899 work clothing and river gear
community-life composition rather than posed spectacle
style_modifiers.palette: river blue, gravel gray, basket tan, pine green, smoke gray, work-cloth brown, salmon dull silver, and late sun gold.

text_rendering.instructions: No readable signs, labels, writing, or invented Native text. Any paper or crate marks must be indistinct.

negative_prompt: copy of commercial game characters, missions, towns, or logos
cropped faces or isolated trophy portrait

self_check: Reads as PS3-era western game art at square page-card scale.
The people, river, salmon gear, baskets, pack animals, and traveler are fully readable.
Native presence is ordinary, respectful, community-centered, and grounded in place.
No invented sacred detail, fake language, costume spectacle, or modern object appears.
No copied game characters, readable modern text, or inherited non-western symbols.
